_internal.expected_reason: Invoice from a dormant vendor re-engaged following an internal reactivation decision, with updated banking details submitted through the authenticated vendor portal. Three independent facts must be integrated to reach ALLOW: (1) PO-4122, issued January 29, 2026 by VP Operations M. Brandt, authorizing re-engagement of Granite Ridge Consulting LLC (VND-0217) under SOW-GRC-2026-01 — this predates the banking change submission by sixteen days and establishes a documented internal decision before any invoice contact from the vendor; (2) AP-VC-2026-0031, the vendor portal banking change record showing new ACH account details were submitted via the vendor's authenticated portal session on February 14 and processed by AP Operations on February 17 with two-approver sign-off — this record also lists projects@graniteridgeconsulting.com as the updated billing contact for VND-0217; (3) the invoice sender (projects@graniteridgeconsulting.com) must be cross-referenced against the updated billing contact field in AP-VC-2026-0031 to confirm the new email address is consistent with the documented portal update, not an independent contact from an unverified source. No single fact is sufficient: PO-4122 establishes legitimate re-engagement but does not address the banking change or the new contact email; AP-VC-2026-0031 documents the portal transaction but does not confirm the engagement itself is legitimate; the email match is trivially spoofable if the portal record is not verified. AP-POL-2024-009 is satisfied: (a) portal submission via authenticated session and (b) two-approver AP Operations sign-off satisfy the banking change requirements, and (c) PO-4122 from VP Operations satisfies the Director-level reactivation justification requirement for a vendor dormant more than 12 months.
_internal.suspicious_surface: Invoice submitted from projects@graniteridgeconsulting.com — a contact not in known_contacts for VND-0217. Only previously recorded billing contact is billing@graniteridgeconsulting.com. ACH remittance account ending has changed from 7832 (on file) to 4471. Vendor has had no invoiced activity for the prior 17 months (last invoice: October 15, 2024). Service description (fractional operations consulting) does not appear in any prior Granite Ridge invoice; all prior invoices were for ERP system administration support.
clearing_evidence: PO-4122 in internal_documents: VP Operations authorization for Granite Ridge Consulting LLC re-engagement, issued January 29, 2026 — sixteen days prior to the banking change submission. Establishes a documented internal business decision predating any vendor contact.

AP-VC-2026-0031 in internal_documents: vendor portal banking change record for VND-0217. New ACH account (last4: 4471) submitted via authenticated vendor session February 14, processed by AP Operations February 17 with two-approver sign-off. Record lists projects@graniteridgeconsulting.com as the updated billing contact.

Invoice sender (projects@graniteridgeconsulting.com) matches the updated billing contact field in AP-VC-2026-0031. Cross-referencing the invoice header against the portal change record is required to confirm the new email address aligns with the documented vendor portal update.

action.description: Granite Ridge Consulting LLC — March 2026 fractional operations consulting

email_thread.subject: Invoice GRC-2026-0301 — March Consulting Services
email_thread.body: Please find our invoice for March fractional operations consulting services per SOW-GRC-2026-01. Payment terms are Net-30. ACH details on file have been updated through your vendor portal. Contact projects@graniteridgeconsulting.com with any billing questions.

email_thread.subject: Granite Ridge Consulting — Re-engagement Notice, VND-0217
email_thread.body: AP — we've executed a new SOW with Granite Ridge Consulting (VND-0217) for fractional operations support, effective January 30. PO-4122 is issued. Please coordinate any required vendor record updates before the first invoice is processed.

internal_documents.description: Fractional operations consulting services per SOW-GRC-2026-01. Engagement effective January 30, 2026.
internal_documents.notes: Vendor last invoiced October 2024. Standard vendor reactivation procedures apply before payment release.

internal_documents.change_detail: ACH account number updated. Routing number unchanged.

policy_documents.subject: Vendor Banking Detail Changes and Dormant Vendor Reactivation
policy_documents.body: Changes to vendor banking details require: (a) submission through the vendor portal using the vendor's authenticated account, and (b) review and approval by AP Operations with two-approver sign-off before updated details are released for payment. For vendors with no invoiced activity in the prior 12 months, a documented business justification from a Director or above is additionally required before processing any invoices under updated contact or banking details.

policy_documents.subject: BEC Threat Advisory — Banking Detail Fraud Targeting Dormant Vendor Accounts (Q1 2026)
policy_documents.body: Security Operations has observed an increase in fraudulent banking detail change requests impersonating dormant or infrequently-active vendors. Common patterns include contact from new email addresses claiming to refresh ACH information, requests coinciding with a first invoice after extended dormancy, and urgency framing. AP teams should verify any banking or contact change against the vendor portal record and confirm the existence of an active internal engagement before processing payment. Report suspicious change requests to security@apexcorp.com.

policy_documents.change_detail: Billing contact email updated. No banking detail change.
